name: Peñarroya & Peña Negra - circular desde Estella-Lizarra
name_original: Peñarroya & Peña Negra – circular desde Estella-Lizarra
region_intro: Hiking around Estella/Lizarra offers diverse terrain, situated amidst mountains like Montejurra and Peñaguda, and defined by the meander of the Ega River. The region features varied landscapes, from the karst massif and beech forests of Urbasa-Andia Natural Park to the historic paths of the Camino de Santiago. Hikers can explore a mix of woodlands, river valleys, and open mountain areas. Best hiking trails around Estella/Lizarra The most popular hiking route is Ruta del Zumaque – circular from Estella-Lizarra through the Parque de los Desvelados, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 8 minutes to complete, passing through a unique open-air art museum. Another top favourite among local hikers is Peñarroya & Peña Negra - circular desde Estella-Lizarra, a moderate 6.4 miles (10.3 km) path. This route offers views of the surrounding hills and natural landscapes near the town. Local hikers also love the ConventoSantoDomingo – Iglesia de San Pedro de la Rúa loop from Estella-Lizarra, a 3.9 miles (6.2 km) trail leading through historical sites and urban landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 40 minutes. Hiking around Estella/Lizarra is defined by mountainous surroundings, river valleys, and the diverse ecosystems of Urbasa-Andia Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Estella/Lizarra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from around 50 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Estella/Lizarra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Estella/Lizarra?
A: There are over 40 hiking routes around Estella/Lizarra, catering to various skill levels. These include easy walks, moderate hikes, and more challenging ascents through the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Estella/Lizarra?
A: The terrain around Estella/Lizarra is quite varied. You can expect mountainous surroundings, river valleys along the Ega River, and the unique karst massif and beech forests of Urbasa-Andia Natural Park. Trails also pass through vineyards, olive groves, and historic paths like the Camino de Santiago.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are 11 easy trails around Estella/Lizarra. The region offers many accessible paths, particularly in areas like Tierras de Iranzu, which are known for their easy-to-do trails. For a moderate option that's still very popular, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968744121/ruta-del-zumaque-circular-from-estella-lizarra-through-the-parque-de-los-desvelados" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta del Zumaque&lt;/a&gt;, which is a circular route passing through the unique Parque de los Desvelados.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Estella/Lizarra?
A: Absolutely. Many routes around Estella/Lizarra are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e969949470/penarroya-pena-negra-circular-desde-estella-lizarra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroya & Peña Negra - circular desde Estella-Lizarra&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic views of the surrounding hills.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature waterfalls or unique natural sights?
A: Yes, the region is home to the stunning &lt;strong&gt;Source of the Urederra River&lt;/strong&gt;, located within the Urbasa-Andia Natural Park, known for its crystal-clear turquoise waters. Another unique natural sight is the &lt;strong&gt;Parque de los Desvelados&lt;/strong&gt;, an open-air museum with giant skulls sculpted into tree branches, which you can experience on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968744121/ruta-del-zumaque-circular-from-estella-lizarra-through-the-parque-de-los-desvelados" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta del Zumaque&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see while hiking in Estella/Lizarra?
A: Estella/Lizarra is rich in history, particularly as a key stop on the Camino de Santiago. You can explore historical sites within the town itself, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7387615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iglesia de San Pedro de la Rúa&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15000803" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;ConventoSantoDomingo – Iglesia de San Pedro de la Rúa loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great way to combine natural beauty with historical exploration.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Estella/Lizarra?
A: The hiking trails in Estella/Lizarra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the blend of natural beauty with historical charm, and the variety of routes suitable for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or panoramic vistas on the trails?
A: Yes, the mountainous surroundings of Estella/Lizarra offer several viewpoints. Within the Urbasa-Andia Natural Park, you'll find breathtaking panoramic views from its vantage points, including astounding views of Mount Beriain. You can also visit specific viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4108046" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Sierra de Lokiz&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4632126" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;MiradorDeLazkua&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is Estella/Lizarra a good base for long-distance hiking?
A: Yes, Estella/Lizarra is a significant stop on the &lt;strong&gt;Camino de Santiago (French Way)&lt;/strong&gt;, making it an excellent base for long-distance hikers. The town provides services and accommodation for pilgrims. Additionally, the &lt;strong&gt;Vasco-Navarro Railway Greenway&lt;/strong&gt;, a 123.5 km trail, connects Estella with Bergara, offering another long-distance option.

Q: What is the best time of year for hiking in Estella/Lizarra?
A: The softened climate of Estella/Lizarra, protected by surrounding mountains, generally makes it pleasant for hiking. Spring and autumn are typically ideal, offering mild temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summer can be warm, but higher elevation trails in areas like Urbasa-Andia Natural Park provide cooler options.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by unique attractions like wine fountains?
A: Yes, the region is known for its unique attractions related to the Camino de Santiago. You can find the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/718255" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wine Fountain — Bodegas Irache&lt;/a&gt;, a popular stop for pilgrims, located near Estella/Lizarra. Another interesting spot is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/382711" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Irache Fountain&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there options for more challenging or difficult hikes?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers, there are 17 difficult trails around Estella/Lizarra. These routes often involve more significant ascents and require good fitness, sure-footedness, and sturdy shoes. The mountainous surroundings, including peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4428728" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montejurra&lt;/a&gt;, provide opportunities for more demanding adventures.
tips: Along with Peña Negra and Sernariaga, Peñarroya is one of the highest peaks in the area, over 500 meters above sea level. and you will have beautiful panoramic views of the entire Ega river valley and Montejurra in front.
